translation_unit_protected_source_text: Subsequently, we applied the conditions from entry 1, which rep- resented the best reaction conditions, to investigate the substrate scope (Table 2). In a typical procedure, the biaryl compound (such as biaryl, diaryl ether, diaryl thioether, and diarylamine), a ketone (such as flu- orenone, acridinone, xanthenone, anthraquinone, and other diary- lketones), and an appropriate amount of solvent (e.g., 1,4-dioxane, acetic acid, DMF) were placed in a round-bottom flask or a Schlenk flask (wrapped in aluminum foil to avoid side reactions under light condi- tions) [26]. Then, the catalyst HNTf₂ [[FORMULA_1]] ) was added. The Table 2
